rationale: All three lenses ran clean; the implementation is correct. One blocking finding (CR-001): the two new tests do not explicitly capture or assert the return value of resolver.resolve(), leaving spec criterion 3 (resolve() returns a falsy value) partially verified — behavior is correct but the explicit assertion is absent. Fix is trivial: add `result = resolver.resolve(...)` and `assert not result` to both new tests. Five blind-lens observations were dismissed as intentional design (fall-through is the fix), existing tested behaviour, or a prompt-transcription artifact (actual code has resolver.resolve, not resolver.solve). Standards and security clean.

business_review.item: Replace ReAuthenticationRequired raise with _invalidate_stale_discovered_credential + return False in _resolve_discovered_server
business_review.notes: resolver.py lines 320-322: exact replacement present in diff
Diff does not touch line 202; only _resolve_discovered_server block changed

business_review.item: No other files change (no schema migrations, no new env vars, no API changes)
business_review.notes: Diff touches only resolver.py and test_resolver.py

business_review.item: No _is_discovered_auth_config_id guard needed — method only reached for discovered servers by construction
business_review.notes: No guard added; spec confirms none needed and diff confirms none present

business_review.item: Test: _invalidate_stale_discovered_credential is called when ReAuthenticationRequired raised
Test: resolve() does NOT raise MCPAuthenticationRequiredException (no SESSION_EXPIRED)
business_review.notes: Both new tests call resolver.resolve() with no pytest.raises wrapper; any exception would fail the test

business_review.item: Test: resolve() returns a falsy value (falls through, allowing discovery to run)
business_review.notes: Neither new test captures or asserts the return value of resolver.resolve(); fall-through is verified only through side-effects (auth_headers == {}, no exception)

business_review.item: Regression guard: existing tests for configured-flow ReAuthenticationRequired (line 202 path) must continue passing
business_review.notes: Out of diff scope; verified by running the full test suite (47 passed)

standards_review.notes: Commit 'EPMCDME-13049: Fix discovered OAuth2 MCP re-auth loop on invalid_grant' matches EPMCDME-NNNNN: Capital description format
3 insertions / 6 deletions; mirrors existing TokenRefreshError pattern exactly; no new abstractions or complexity
return False triggers discovery probe which enforces re-authentication; no bypass created; invalidate_if_stale removes the dead credential

findings.title: resolve() return value not asserted in new tests
findings.problem: Neither test_discovered_flow_invalid_grant_invalidates_credential_and_falls_through nor test_discovered_flow_invalid_grant_falls_through_even_if_invalidate_raises captures or asserts the return value of resolver.resolve(), leaving spec criterion 3 only partially verified.
findings.impact: A future change that alters the return value while preserving side-effects would not be caught; the spec explicitly requires this assertion.
findings.recommendation: In both new tests, replace `resolver.resolve(server_config, "user-1", execution_context)` with `result = resolver.resolve(server_config, "user-1", execution_context)` and add `assert not result` (or `assert result is False`) on the next line.
